subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the storyline and main events in 'The Cherry Tree'
Analyse the relationship between Rakesh and his grandfather
Identify the challenges faced by the cherry tree and how it survives
Interpret the themes of patience, perseverance, and human–nature connection
Develop reading comprehension and analytical thinking
Enhance vocabulary and grammar through text-based exercises

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Nature and Tree Planting
topics_covered: Importance of trees in our environment
Personal experiences with planting or caring for plants
Introduction to the story and its setting
teaching_strategy: Teacher-led questioning based on pre-reading activities
Brief introduction to the author Ruskin Bond
student_activities: Share experiences of planting seeds or trees
List fruit trees found in their local area

lesson_plan.title: Beginning of the Story
topics_covered: Rakesh’s life with his grandfather in Mussoorie
Buying cherries and planting the seed
Grandfather’s advice about luck and effort
teaching_strategy: Guided reading of the first section of the story
Teacher explanation of important events and vocabulary
student_activities: Explain why Rakesh decided to plant the cherry seed

lesson_plan.title: Growth of the Cherry Tree
topics_covered: Discovery of the young cherry plant
Care and watering of the plant
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation with examples from the text
student_activities: Create a simple timeline showing the tree’s growth
Write a few sentences describing the early growth of the tree

lesson_plan.title: Challenges Faced by the Cherry Tree
topics_covered: Tree being cut accidentally by a woman
teaching_strategy: Discussion about obstacles faced by the tree
Teacher-led explanation of resilience in nature
student_activities: Identify cause-and-effect relationships in the story
Discuss why the cherry tree did not die

lesson_plan.title: Blossoms, Fruits, and the Climax
topics_covered: The cherry tree growing taller than Rakesh
Birds visiting and eating the fruit
student_activities: Describe the cherry tree using adjectives
Discuss why the tree is special to Rakesh

lesson_plan.title: Theme, Message, and Language Activities
topics_covered: Vocabulary and grammar exercises from the text
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of the story’s theme
student_activities: Write the moral of the story
Reflect on how caring for nature can benefit humans

teaching_aids: Chart showing stages of plant growth
teacher_tip: Encourage students to connect the story with real-life experiences of planting or caring for trees. This will help them understand the themes of patience and the human–nature relationship.
